rationale: Both independently converge on the same real diagnosis (PR #4834's _level2_sleeping guard vs. a stale test still calling sleep(level=2)→wake_up()), matching yenuo26's thread attribution to #4834, and both cite plausible file/line evidence. But the actual thread never confirms resolution — its last action is Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run — so a confident 'close, already fixed' verdict outruns what's established. X hedges appropriately ('confirm CI was on the right commit and re-trigger if needed') and pings the same people the thread was waiting on, aligning with the real next step. Y asserts closure as settled fact, invents unverifiable specifics (a PR title, a merge date, three named regression tests) not evidenced anywhere, and never engages the outstanding CI-trigger request, making it less complete relative to the reporter's actual situation despite being more verbose.
